name: Pier Code

form_factor.comment: Terminal-native CLI installed from the official shell installer. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://www.piercode.com/.

released_in.comment: Changelog lists v0.1.0 as the first stable release on 17 Jun 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest public changelog entry is v0.4.1 on 7 Jul 2026, adding saved plan files and one-step transition from plan approval into Auto mode.

rules.comment: Pier inspects repository context. Verified locally in pier 0.4.1 model catalog and binary strings: it looks for AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, and related contributor instruction files.

skills.comment: Local pier 0.4.1 exposes plugin management and stable plugin features, with bundled skill support visible in the binary, but public docs do not yet describe a user-facing SKILL.md system.

hooks.comment: Local pier 0.4.1 feature list reports hooks as stable and the CLI exposes hook-trust bypass controls. Public Pier docs do not yet document the hook file format.

mcp_servers.comment: Verified locally: `pier mcp` manages external MCP servers with list/get/add/remove/login/logout subcommands.

custom_commands.comment: Public docs and local top-level help show built-in commands, but no documented custom command directory or slash-command authoring format was found.

goal_command.comment: Local pier 0.4.1 feature list reports goals as stable. Public docs do not yet spell out the slash-command surface.

subagents.comment: Local pier 0.4.1 feature list reports multi_agent as stable and the binary exposes subagent events. Public docs are still sparse.

monitor.comment: Changelog documents listing/stopping background terminals. This covers background command visibility, but public docs do not describe a general event-driven monitor primitive.

model_selection.comment: Verified locally: `pier` and `pier exec` accept `-m/--model`; model catalog includes pier-hybrid, sarvam-105b, sarvam-30b, and Max-plan frontier models.

approval_mode.comment: Verified locally: `pier` supports `-a/--ask-for-approval` values untrusted, on-failure, on-request, and never, plus the explicit dangerous bypass flag.

sandbox_mode.comment: Verified locally: `pier` supports `-s/--sandbox` values read-only, workspace-write, and danger-full-access, plus a `pier sandbox` command.

resume.comment: Verified locally: `pier resume` resumes a previous interactive session by id/name or via picker/--last; `pier exec resume` supports non-interactive resume.

continue.comment: Verified locally: `pier resume --last` and `pier exec resume --last` continue the most recent recorded session.

non_interactive.comment: Verified locally: `pier exec` runs tasks non-interactively, and `pier review` runs code review non-interactively.

output_format.comment: Verified locally: `pier exec --json` prints events as JSONL and `--output-last-message` writes the final assistant message to a file.

statusline.comment: No public Pier documentation for configurable statusline customization was found in this run.

telemetry.comment: Security page says telemetry is user-controlled and defaults favor privacy.

custom_model_provider.comment: Max plan exposes frontier providers and local pier 0.4.1 supports `--oss` plus lmstudio/ollama local providers. Public docs do not yet document arbitrary OpenAI-compatible provider configuration.

pricing.comment: Pricing page lists Pro at $5/month, Max at $20/month, and $5 usage packs.

some_free_usage.comment: No free tier was listed on the public pricing page reviewed in this run.

no_account_required.comment: The CLI is local-first and local-only mode exists, but paid/cloud features require account access. Local pier 0.4.1 also exposes OSS local-provider flags.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Pier Max unlocks frontier providers through Pier billing; public docs reviewed did not show BYOK or routing through a user's external provider subscription.

hosted_agent.comment: Changelog documents remote execution where Pier runs the agent in the cloud, clones the repo, works on it, and pushes a branch.

transcripts.comment: Verified locally: Pier persists sessions for resume/archive/delete/fork flows under its local Pier state.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: No public keybinding customization documentation was found in this run.

theme_customization.comment: No public theme customization documentation was found in this run.

auto_save.comment: Changelog documents plan mode saving the plan to a file as it is written. Broader auto-save controls are not public yet.

debugging_output.comment: Verified locally: `pier doctor --json`, `pier debug models`, and `pier debug prompt-input` expose diagnostic output.

context_window.comment: Local model catalog exposes context window sizes for Pier models, but no public user-configurable context-window setting was found.

code_formatting.comment: No built-in formatter/linter integration documentation was found in this run.

git_integration.comment: Remote execution pushes changes to a branch; local CLI also exposes `pier apply` for applying the latest diff. No full PR workflow docs were found.

performance_mode.comment: Pier Hybrid routes between fast and deep workers, and local model catalog lists fast service tiers. No general performance-mode settings docs were found.

ui_customization.comment: No public UI customization documentation was found in this run.

notes: Pier Code is a terminal coding agent from Alphabench LLP. Added to agent-launch as `--agent pier` targeting the `pier` binary; installer verified on this machine with pier 0.4.1 under `~/.pier/bin/pier`.
